synopsis: Ten years after Troy's fall, Odysseus struggles homeward past monsters and gods to the wife and son who still await him.

title: The Paypal Wars: Battles with eBay, the Media, the Mafia, and the Rest of Planet Earth
author: Eric M Jackson
synopsis: An insider's account of PayPal's scrappy fight to survive fraud, rivals, and eBay on its way to reinventing online money.

title: The Pickwick Papers
author: Charles Dickens
synopsis: The genial Mr. Pickwick and his club ramble across England, tumbling through misadventures, lawsuits, and the comic loyalty of Sam Weller.

title: The Pirate
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: In the remote Shetland Isles, a shipwrecked stranger who proves to be a pirate captain unsettles the loves of two sisters.

title: The Political Brain
author: Drew Westen
synopsis: A psychologist argues that elections are won through emotion, not reason, and faults Democrats for appealing to the head over the heart.

title: The Powers That Be
author: David Halberstam
synopsis: A sweeping history of how CBS, Time, the Washington Post, and the Los Angeles Times grew into empires that shaped American politics.

title: The Prophet
author: Khalil Gibran
synopsis: On the eve of his departure, the sage Almustafa answers a town's questions with poetic counsel on love, work, sorrow, and death.

title: The Rape of the Lock
author: Alexander Pope
synopsis: A stolen lock of hair ignites a mock-epic war among the fashionable belles and beaux of England.

title: The Razor's Edge
author: W. Somerset Maugham
synopsis: A young American, scarred by the Great War, forsakes wealth and convention to wander in search of meaning.

title: The Red and the Black
author: Stendhal
synopsis: An ambitious carpenter's son schemes his way through the church and the salons of Restoration France.

title: The Religion of Nature Delineated
author: William Wollaston
synopsis: A moral philosophy grounding right and wrong in whether our actions affirm or deny the truth of things.

title: The Religions of Japan
author: William Elliot Griffis
synopsis: A survey of Shinto, Buddhism, and Confucianism as they shaped the faith and daily life of Japan.

title: The Renaissance
author: Walter Pater
synopsis: Essays on the art and poetry of the Renaissance that urge the reader to burn with a hard, gemlike flame.

title: The Romance of the Forest
author: Ann Radcliffe
synopsis: A persecuted young woman takes refuge in a ruined abbey where buried family secrets slowly close in around her.

title: The Saints' Everlasting Rest
author: Richard Baxter
synopsis: A Puritan pastor's meditation urging the faithful toward the eternal rest that awaits them in heaven.

title: The Satyricon
author: Petronius
synopsis: The disreputable misadventures of a wandering rogue and his boy lover, centered on a vulgar freedman's lavish banquet.

title: The Sea
author: Barry Cornwall
synopsis: A rolling hymn to the open sea, which the poet claims as his wild home, his joy, and his ever-free element.

title: The Seasons
author: James Thomson
synopsis: A four-part poem tracing the turning year through spring, summer, autumn, and winter across the English countryside.

title: The Shaughraun
author: Dion Boucicault
synopsis: A merry Irish vagabond schemes to rescue a Fenian exile and outwit an informer in a melodrama of old Ireland.

title: The Sketch Book of Geoffrey Crayon
author: Washington Irving
synopsis: A genial traveler's miscellany of English sketches and American tales, home to Rip Van Winkle and Sleepy Hollow.

title: The Sound and Fury
author: William Faulkner
synopsis: The Compson family's decline told in fractured voices, beginning with the wordless grief of an idiot son.

title: The Spirit of the Laws
author: Montesquieu
synopsis: A sweeping comparison of governments arguing that liberty depends on separating legislative, executive, and judicial power.

title: The Swiss Family Robinson
author: Johann David Wyss
synopsis: A shipwrecked pastor and his family build an ingenious island home from the salvage of their wrecked ship.

title: The Tattler
author: John Addison
synopsis: A gentleman's periodical of essays on manners, morals, and the coffeehouse life of early eighteenth-century London.

title: The Tour of Doctor Syntax in Search of the Picturesque
author: William Combe
synopsis: A bumbling clergyman-schoolmaster sets off on horseback in comic pursuit of picturesque scenery and easy fortune.

title: The Vanity of Human Wishes
author: Samuel Johnson
synopsis: A grave survey of humanity's futile ambitions, showing how wealth, power, and fame all end in ruin.

title: The Way of All Flesh
author: Samuel Butler
synopsis: Four generations of a Victorian family expose the quiet tyrannies of respectable religion and paternal love.

title: The Whole Duty of Man
author: Richard Allestree
synopsis: A devotional manual laying out the ordinary Christian's duties to God, to neighbor, and to himself.

title: The Wide, Wide World
author: Susan Warner
synopsis: An orphaned girl, sent from her dying mother into hard hands, learns to endure through piety and tears.

title: The Winter's Tale
author: William Shakespeare
synopsis: A king's sudden jealousy destroys his family, until time and a living statue restore what he had lost.

title: The Wizard of Oz
author: L Frank Baum
synopsis: A Kansas girl swept to a magic land follows a golden road, gathering odd companions on her way home.

title: Theory of the Leisure Class
author: Thorstein Veblen
synopsis: An economist dissects how the wealthy signal status through conspicuous consumption and deliberate waste rather than usefulness.

title: Things Fall Apart
author: Chinua Achebe
synopsis: Okonkwo, a proud Igbo warrior, watches his world and his standing unravel as British colonizers and missionaries arrive in his Nigerian village.

title: Toussant l'Ouverture
author: Wendel Phillips
synopsis: An abolitionist orator hails the self-freed slave who led Haiti's revolution as a soldier and statesman greater than Napoleon.

title: Travels with a Donkey
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: A restless young writer treks through the Cévennes mountains with only a stubborn donkey named Modestine for company.

title: Treason: Liberal Treachery from the Cold War to the War on Terrorism
author: Ann Coulter
synopsis: A combative polemic arguing that American liberals have betrayed the nation from the Cold War through the war on terror.

title: Treatise on the Law of Corporations
author: Stewart Kyd
synopsis: The first English-language treatise on corporate law, defining what a corporation is and how one may be created, governed, and dissolved.

title: Undine and Sintram
author: Friedrich de la Motte Fouqué
synopsis: A water nymph marries a knight to win a human soul, only to be betrayed by his wandering heart.

title: Urn Burial
author: Thomas Browne
synopsis: Roman funeral urns unearthed in Norfolk prompt a sweeping meditation on death, memory, and the vanity of earthly monuments.

title: Valentine and Orson
author: Anonymous
synopsis: Twin brothers separated at birth — one raised at court, the other by a bear in the forest — reunite in chivalric adventure.

title: Valley of the Dolls
author: Jacqueline Susann
synopsis: Three ambitious women chase fame in New York and Hollywood, propped up by pills and worn down by the men around them.

title: Voyage autour de ma Chambre
author: Xavier de Maistre
synopsis: Confined to his room for forty-two days, an officer turns the journey from bed to armchair into a witty philosophical travelogue.

title: Walden
author: Henry David Thoreau
synopsis: A man retreats to a cabin by a Massachusetts pond to live deliberately and strip life down to its essentials.

title: Waverley
author: Walter Scott
synopsis: A dreamy young English officer is swept into the doomed Jacobite rising of 1745 in the Scottish Highlands.

title: What's the Matter with Kansas
author: Thomas Frank
synopsis: An argument that working-class Americans have been lured into voting against their own economic interests by cultural grievances.

title: Wilhelm Meister's Apprenticeship
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
synopsis: A merchant's son abandons commerce for the theater, drifting through love and illusion toward a hard-won maturity.

title: Windsor Forest
author: Alexander Pope
synopsis: A pastoral poem celebrating the English countryside and the peace of Queen Anne's reign.

title: Wonderful Ethiopians of the Ancient Cushite Empire
author: Drusilla D Houston
synopsis: A sweeping history arguing that a great Cushite civilization of black Africa shaped the ancient world from Ethiopia to India.

title: the Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
synopsis: Evolution seen from the gene's point of view, where organisms are survival machines built to propagate their DNA.

title: À Rebours
author: Joris-Karl Huysmans
synopsis: A jaded aristocrat withdraws from society into a private world of rare perfumes, art, and exquisite artificial sensations.

title: Émaux et Camées
author: Théophile Gautier
synopsis: A collection of finely chiseled poems that treat verse as sculpture, pursuing beauty for its own sake.

title: Articles of Belief and Acts of Religion
author: Benjamin Franklin
synopsis: The young printer drafts a private liturgy and personal creed for his own solitary worship of a benevolent God.

title: Before an Audience
author: Nathan Sheppard
synopsis: Force of will, not gesture or elocution, is what truly commands a speaker's audience.

title: Belsham's Essays
author: William Belsham
synopsis: Essays ranging across philosophy, history, and letters, defending human liberty against the doctrine of necessity.

title: Brideshead
author: Evelyn Waugh
synopsis: Charles Ryder recalls his enchantment with an aristocratic Catholic family and its slow, grace-haunted decline.

title: Buy Low, Sell High, Collect Early, and Pay Late: The Manager's Guide to Financial Survival
author: Richard I. Levin
synopsis: A manager's plain-spoken guide to reading the numbers and surviving the game of corporate finance.

title: Caesar's Wars
author: Julius Caesar
synopsis: Caesar's own terse account of his conquest of Gaul and the civil war that made him Rome's master.

title: Children of the Sun
author: George Wells Parker
synopsis: Parker marshals early anthropology to argue that Greek and Mesopotamian civilization sprang from African roots.

title: Confession of General Bonaparte to he Abbe Maury
author: Jean Sarrazin
synopsis: An exiled general puts imagined confessions in Napoleon's mouth, a bitter satire on imperial ambition.

title: Contes de Daudet
author: Alphonse Daudet
synopsis: Tales of Provence and wartime France, tender, ironic, and lit with Southern sun.

title: Crowds
author: Gerald Stanley Lee
synopsis: Lee reads modern democracy as one vast, hopeful crowd and asks how to make it good and beautiful.

title: Crown of Wild Olive
author: John Ruskin
synopsis: Four Ruskin lectures on work, traffic, war, and what a nation owes to its own soul.

title: Dresden, History, Stage and Gallery
author: Mary Endell
synopsis: A cultured tour of Dresden's history, its opera and theater, and the treasures of its picture gallery.

title: Edda
author: Snorri Sturluson
synopsis: Snorri gathers the Norse myths and the craft of skaldic verse into the great handbook of the North.

title: Feudal and Modern Japan
author: Arthur May Knapp
synopsis: Knapp explains how Japan cast off feudalism and remade itself into a modern power within a single generation.

title: Friendship's Offering
synopsis: A Christmas gift-book annual gathering poems, songs, and prose legends from Byron, Southey, and other fashionable authors of the day.

title: History Philosophically Illustrated
author: George Miller
synopsis: Miller traces the philosophical causes behind European events from the fall of Rome to the French Revolution.

title: How Plants Grow
author: Asa Gray
synopsis: Asa Gray's simple introduction to botany for young students, long a standard American school text.

title: Japan in Transition
author: Stafford Ransome
synopsis: Ransome measures Japan's rapid progress, policy, and methods in the years after its victory over China.

title: Miscellaneous Essays
author: George Saintsbury
synopsis: Saintsbury's critical essays range over English and French prose style, Baudelaire, Renan, and the present state of the novel.

title: Poppies
author: Natsume Soseki
synopsis: Six young men and women navigate love and ambition in Meiji Japan's collision of tradition and modernity.

title: Princess Mary's Gift Book
author: various (anthology, ed. for Queen Mary's charity)
synopsis: Stories and poems by Barrie, Kipling, and Doyle, sold to fund Christmas gifts for Britain's wartime soldiers and sailors.

title: Psychology
author: Daniel Putnam
synopsis: An elementary school textbook of mental and moral science, surveying perception, memory, emotion, and will.

title: Talk to Students on the Art of Study
author: Frank Cramer
synopsis: A biologist's practical counsel to students on how to concentrate, reason, and learn effectively.

title: The Gist of Japan
author: R. B. Peery
synopsis: A missionary surveys Japan's islands, people, and religions, and the progress of Christian missions among them.

title: The Infallibility of Human Judgment
author: Lyons
synopsis: Lyons proposes a new art of reasoning that reduces every dispute to self-evident propositions to settle truth.

title: The Midnight Bell
author: Francis Lathom
synopsis: Fleeing his father's murder, a young Saxon count is drawn back to a haunted castle whose bell tolls each midnight.

title: The Mysterious Warning
author: Eliza Parsons
synopsis: A spectral voice warns young Ferdinand against his brother, launching a Gothic tangle of disinheritance, murder, and buried family sin.

title: The Necromancer of the Black Forest
author: Ludwig Flammenberg (Carl Friedrich Kahlert)
synopsis: Interlocking tales of banditry and seeming sorcery in the Black Forest circle a mysterious necromancer whose hauntings prove all too human.

title: The Orphan of the Rhine
author: Eleanor Sleath
synopsis: An orphaned girl and her protector flee across Italy through haunted castles as a hidden noble parentage and villainous schemes unravel.

title: The Principles of Ethics
author: Herbert Spencer
synopsis: Spencer grounds morality in evolution, deriving the laws of right conduct from biology, psychology, and the survival of society.

title: The Working Principles of Rhetoric
author: John Franklin Genung
synopsis: Genung's exhaustive college manual anatomizes effective writing into invention, style, and structure as teachable working principles.

title: Thinking and Learning to Think
author: Nathan C. Schaeffer
synopsis: Schaeffer examines attention, memory, and reasoning to argue that education's true aim is teaching pupils to think, not memorize.

title: Voyages
author: James Cook
synopsis: Captain Cook's own journals of Pacific exploration, charting Tahiti, New Zealand, and the search for a great southern continent.

title: Works and Days
author: Hesiod
synopsis: Hesiod counsels his idle brother on honest labor, the farmer's calendar, and the ages of man in a world where the gods watch over justice.

title: Writing and Speaking
author: Charles Sears Baldwin
synopsis: Baldwin's rhetoric text treats composition and oratory together, teaching how ideas become clear, persuasive writing and speech.

title: Time Traveler
author: Norman and Jeanne MacKenzie
synopsis: A full-length biography of H.G. Wells, tracing the draper's apprentice who became a prophet of science fiction and social reform.

title: College Friends
author: Edmondo De Amicis
synopsis: De Amicis's nostalgic reminiscence of his years as a cadet at the military college of Modena, recalling the friendships, hardships, and youthful ardor of army-school life.

title: Essays
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
synopsis: Emerson's First Series preaches individual conscience and the divinity within each soul, from Self-Reliance to The Over-Soul.

title: The Essays
author: Michel de Montaigne
synopsis: Montaigne turns his skeptical gaze on himself, wandering through fear, friendship, and death to ask, 'What do I know?'
